title: Estimating Dynamic Panel Models: Backing out the Nickell Bias

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is a 2017 Staff Report from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York authored by Jerry A. Hausman and Maxim L. Pinkovskiy proposing a novel estimator for dynamic panel models that addresses the Nickell bias through calculating bias in first-order conditions as a function of the autoregressive parameter. The source content is extremely thin, consisting primarily of a brief abstract and website navigation elements, with the full paper available only in PDF.
key_points: Authors: Jerry A. Hausman and Maxim L. Pinkovskiy
Published October 2017 as Staff Report Number 824
Proposes novel estimator solving the failure of strict exogeneity by calculating bias in first-order conditions
Also proposes a general method for including predetermined variables in fixed-effects panel regressions
Full paper available only in PDF format
why_it_matters: This is an econometric methodology paper with no direct relevance to current market structure or macro catalysts. It is a historical research publication from 2017 that would not inform near-term trading or policy decisions. The TOPIC/TARGET classification as 'market structure' in the source metadata is incorrect - this is panel data econometrics research.
